steps.title: Compare study demands
steps.content: The passage contrasts a simple fitness study with HILDA, which follows data for 25,000 people to find economic well-being trends that take a long time to develop.

steps.title: Explain the funding need
steps.content: Such a longitudinal study costs more because long-term economic trends require data collected over many years. B and C shift to fitness studies, and D speculates that the sample size may be insufficient, which the passage does not claim. The answer is A.
